data.name: Vertical AI infrastructure wins

data.name: On-prem inference for regulated industries
data.brief: Regulatory pressure and unit economics push inference off public cloud for fintech, healthcare, defense.

data.name: Agent-first CRMs displace per-seat SaaS
data.brief: Agents replace workflows; per-seat pricing collapses for sales+support tooling.

data.name: Data quality is the durable AI moat
data.brief: In an era of commodity models, data quality + cleanliness is the lasting differentiator.

data.name: Eval/benchmarking becomes its own category
data.brief: AI eval graduates from internal tools to standalone purchased product.

data.name: Agent-aware security wins
data.brief: Existing security tools are blind to agentic attack surfaces; new category emerges.

data.name: Vertical SaaS + AI shells succeed
data.brief: Layering an AI shell on top of established vertical SaaS unlocks growth. CONTRADICTED in Q4 2025.

data.name: Mobile-edge inference category emerges
data.brief: On-device inference becomes a venture category. Retired Q2 2025 — too constrained by silicon roadmaps.

data.name: Vertical data asymmetry compounds
data.brief: Vertical players accumulate proprietary domain data faster than hyperscalers.

data.name: Customers prefer vertical bundles
data.brief: Mid-market customers in regulated verticals prefer a single vertical-AI vendor over an integrated stack.

data.name: Data sovereignty mandates force on-prem
data.brief: EU AI Act + DORA force regulated industries to keep inference on-prem.

data.name: On-prem inference reaches cost parity
data.brief: On-prem H100 cost-per-token will reach cloud parity by end of 2026.

data.name: Per-seat pricing collapses for sales/support
data.brief: Agents replacing workflows undermine per-seat SaaS pricing for sales and support.

data.name: Agent-human handoffs work reliably
data.brief: Agents can hand off to humans cleanly in CRM workflows.

data.name: Cost-of-failure justifies data quality spend
data.brief: Enterprises will pay for data quality as a separate line item.

data.name: Eval tooling stays fragmented
data.brief: No single hyperscaler ships best-of-class eval; market stays open.

data.name: Agent attack surface grows fast
data.brief: Enterprise agentic deployment outpaces security investment for 18+ months.

data.name: Current security tools blind to agents
data.brief: Existing SIEMs and EDRs do not detect agentic activity.

data.name: Vertical-SaaS AI shells drive product pull
data.brief: Adding AI shells to legacy vertical SaaS unlocks new ARR. CONTRADICTED.

data.name: Helix can maintain 60%+ gross margin
data.brief: Helix's on-prem deployment model sustains gross margins above 60% as it scales.

data.name: Can Helix sustain margins as cloud catches up?
data.description: AWS Bedrock now offers on-prem appliances. Does Helix's margin profile hold?

data.name: Does Axon get enterprise pull vs hobby devs?
data.description: Axon's bottom-up traction is real. But the ARR concentration is in <10K orgs. Need to validate enterprise sales motion.

data.name: Who buys Sigil — CISO or platform eng?
data.description: Sigil's value prop straddles two budgets. Need to know which one closes faster.

data.name: How defensible is Pinion's orchestration layer?
data.description: Pinion's wedge is orchestration across on-prem and cloud. As hyperscalers ship cross-cloud abstractions, what's the moat?

data.name: Should we follow on Aetherbrick's Series B?
data.description: Aetherbrick's churn signal is mixed. Decision needed before June board.

data.name: Is the data quality market big enough?
data.description: Bramble is hitting growth but TAM remains uncertain.
data.answer: Two of the five enterprises we surveyed treat data quality as a renewable line item. Insufficient evidence for $1B TAM yet.

data.name: Will QuireBench hit milestones for Series A?
data.description: QuireBench has 14 months of runway. Series A milestones require 5x ARR.

data.name: Was retiring vertical-shell thesis right?
data.description: Two recent deals contradict our pass on this thesis. Worth re-opening?

data.name: Will data eng or platform team buy data quality?
data.description: Bramble and Canon both target this. Need to know which budget closes the deal.

data.name: Will fintech regs shift in 2026 for Saltline?
data.description: Saltline's TAM expansion depends on DORA enforcement intensity.

data.name: Helix — bull case
data.summary: Helix is the right team at the right time for the on-prem inference wave. Two-time founder with relevant exit, anchor customer mandate, regulatory tailwind.
data.body: Three reasons Helix wins: (1) EU AI Act + DORA enforcement is making on-prem non-negotiable, validated by April €42M fine. (2) Yuki Tanaka is a second-time founder who sold an infra startup to AWS — pattern match. (3) First major customer reference mandates on-prem-only.

data.name: Helix — bear case
data.summary: AWS Bedrock on-prem appliance launched in March. Hyperscalers will undercut Helix's margins before they reach scale.
data.body: Two-pronged risk: (1) AWS Bedrock on-prem will commoditize. (2) Helix's margin assumption (60%+) assumes Helix doesn't have to compete with bundled hyperscaler offerings. The day AWS or Microsoft offers comparable on-prem stack for free with their cloud commits, Helix's economics collapse.

data.name: Axon Eval — IC memo
data.summary: Recommend invest at $13M pre. Strong bottom-up, eval is becoming a product category, Databricks acquihire pattern de-risks distribution outcome.

data.name: Vector Forge — pass rationale
data.summary: Passed because the AWS exclusivity deal preempted the vertical-data-asymmetry argument we'd use to underwrite. We still believe the thesis; this team's path is closed.

data.name: Aetherbrick — follow-on brief
data.summary: Mixed picture. Q1 churn spike is real but concentrated in one customer segment. Recommend partial follow-on at Series B with downside protection.

data.name: Saltline — Q1 recap
data.summary: Outstanding quarter on revenue. CS head departure is the wildcard; reseed CS leadership before Series A.

data.name: On-prem inference — pattern emerging
data.summary: Three independent signals in 90 days (EU AI Act enforcement, Microsoft on-prem GPU SKUs, AWS Bedrock appliance) confirm the on-prem inference pattern.

data.name: Eval as a product — hypothesis
data.summary: Eval will fragment into 3-5 winners by 2028, one of which will be vertical-specialist. Databricks acquihire validates the category.

data.name: Vertical SaaS + AI shell — failure pattern
data.summary: Two recently funded shell-style startups have already retrenched. Pattern: distribution doesn't fix product-market fit.

data.name: Data quality — buyer shifting to platform engineering
data.summary: In five recent diligence calls, four buyers were platform engineering, not data engineering. Reposition Bramble and Canon pitches accordingly.

data.name: Pass on Vector Forge seed
data.rationale: AWS exclusivity deal kills the vertical-data-asymmetry argument we'd underwrite this team on.

data.name: On-prem demand validation protocol
data.body: Before underwriting any on-prem-inference deal, complete: (1) one CIO call at a target customer, (2) one platform engineering call, (3) one finance/CFO call confirming budget authority. All three must agree on-prem-only is non-negotiable.

data.name: Score vertical data moat with 5 customer interviews
data.body: Before underwriting a vertical-AI thesis on a deal, interview 5 in-vertical customers about their willingness to give the startup proprietary data access. Below 3/5 = no moat.

data.name: Pass on vertical-SaaS-with-AI-shell deals absent product-led pull
data.body: Generic AI shells on top of legacy vertical SaaS consistently underperform unless there is independent evidence of product-led pull (free tier signups, organic growth). Distribution does not fix PMF.

data.name: Bias toward second-time infra founders
data.body: Boost second-time founders building infra products by one conviction notch. Tentative — needs more datapoints to ratify.

data.name: Wiki: On-prem inference for regulated industries
data.body:
# On-prem inference for regulated industries

**Status:** active · **Confidence:** high · **Formed:** 2025-01-10

## Why we believe this

Regulatory pressure (EU AI Act, DORA) and unit economics (H100 amortization, data egress) push inference off public cloud for fintech, healthcare, and defense. The bet: there's a category of inference-orchestration platforms that win by being the *trusted on-prem layer* — neither the model vendor nor the hyperscaler.

## Underlying assumptions

1. Data sovereignty mandates force on-prem (high confidence)
2. On-prem inference reaches cost parity by end of 2026 (medium)
3. Helix in particular can maintain 60%+ margin (medium — under review post-AWS-Bedrock)

## Disqualifying signals

- Hyperscalers ship on-prem appliances that close the cost+latency gap (AWS Bedrock March 2026; under review)
- Open-source on-prem stacks (vLLM + similar) commoditize the orchestration layer

## Active bets

- Pinion Infer (portfolio) — Series A
- Helix Runtime (in-diligence) — Series A target close July 2026

data.name: Wiki: On-prem demand validation protocol
data.body:
# On-prem demand validation protocol

**Status:** active · **Confidence:** high · **Kind:** protocol

Before underwriting any on-prem-inference deal, complete:

1. **One CIO call at a regulated-industry target customer.** Not VP Eng — CIO. Question: is on-prem mandated by *internal policy* or just *current preference*?
2. **One ex-hyperscaler infra eng call.** Question: how long until {AWS/Azure/GCP} ships a competitive on-prem SKU for this workload?
3. **One reference call with a current paying customer.** Question: would you switch to a hyperscaler appliance if priced 30% below?

**Why this rule exists:** post-Vector Forge, we recognized that vendor-claimed mandates often turn out to be vendor-claimed *preferences* that fold under price pressure. This protocol forces three independent data points before conviction.

**When to skip:** never. Even on second-time founders.

data.name: Wiki: On-prem inference shift pattern
data.body:
# On-prem inference shift

**Kind:** market-timing · **Confidence:** high

Regulated industries are pushing inference off public cloud — recurring shape across fintech (EU DORA), healthcare (HIPAA + state-level data residency), and EU public sector (AI Act Art. 22). Capital flowing toward on-prem orchestration startups (Pinion, Helix) and away from pure cloud-vendor solutions.

First observed: 2024-Q4 with the first European bank we tracked turning down a Bedrock pilot for a Pinion POC.

Now observed across:
- Helix Runtime — Series A — on-prem inference platform for regulated industries
- Pinion Infer — Series A — inference orchestration across on-prem and cloud
- And tangentially: data-sovereignty signals around mkt-ai-infra and mkt-security

**Promoted to active thesis** (thesis-on-prem-inference, 2025-01-10).
**Driven decisions:** cmt-helix-second-meeting, dec-onprem-thesis-doubledown.
